test.note: Tests cannot run on macOS per services.yaml; validation is through the server Docker stack. This is a known pre-existing constraint, not a new failure.

typecheck.note: mypy reports 1770 errors across 91 files — identical in scope and nature to the pre-existing errors documented in the stablecoin-pricing scrutiny synthesis. None trace to files introduced or modified in the exotic-tokens milestone. Treated as pre-existing per prior synthesis precedent.

lint.output: All done! ✨ 🍰 ✨ 163 files would be left unchanged.

reviewsSummary.note: Round 2 re-run: exotic-tokens-scrutiny-fixes feature is still pending (not completed). No new features introduced since round 1. Prior reviews preserved at .factory/validation/exotic-tokens/scrutiny/reviews/. No new subagents spawned since no fix features were completed.

blockingIssues.description: test_gauge_bucket_detection uses SDAI_USDM_GAUGE (0xcF5136C67fA8A375BaBbDf13c0307EF994b5681D) as test address, but this same address is hardcoded in y/prices/one_to_one.py line 18. check_bucket() evaluates string_matchers (including 'one to one') BEFORE calls_only (including 'curve gauge'), so the test will assert 'curve gauge' but receive 'one to one'. Fix: replace SDAI_USDM_GAUGE in test_gauge_bucket_detection with a gauge address NOT in one_to_one.py.

PoolTogether V4 ticket detection is broken: is_pool_together_v4_ticket() in y/prices/exotic_tokens.py line 168-170 calls has_methods(token, ('getTwab(address,uint32)(uint224,uint32)', 'controller()(address)'), all). has_methods only works for view methods with NO inputs (contracts.py:839). getTwab requires two inputs (address, uint32), so has_methods always returns False for it, making the entire 'pool together v4 ticket' bucket permanently unreachable (dead code). Fix: remove getTwab from the has_methods call; use only controller()(address) (no-input), then separately verify the name pattern matches 'PoolTogether * Ticket'.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add rule: @stuck_coro_debugger must be applied to is_* detection functions as well as get_price_* functions, following the yearn.py pattern (@a_sync.a_sync → @stuck_coro_debugger → @optional_async_diskcache). Detection functions make multiple RPC calls and can stall just as pricing functions can.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: y/prices/exotic_tokens.py is_pickle_pslp, is_pool_together_v4_ticket, is_xpremia, is_xtarot, is_tarot_supply_vault — all missing @stuck_coro_debugger. y/prices/yearn.py:77 has the correct pattern. Two separate reviewers flagged this across erc4626-vault-improvement and exotic-token-parity-and-detection.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note: When writing unit tests for detection functions that use calls_only bucket detection, verify that the chosen test address is NOT also present in string_matchers (especially one_to_one.py MAPPING). Addresses in one_to_one.py will always resolve to 'one to one' bucket before calls_only is evaluated. Test addresses for calls_only buckets must be algorithmically-detectable-only (not hardcoded anywhere).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: curve-gauge-support blocking issue: SDAI_USDM_GAUGE in test_gauge_bucket_detection is also in one_to_one.py line 18, so the test will always fail in CI.
